name: Issyk-Kul Region (Kyrgyzstan)

colors.symbolism: Lake Issyk-Kul, after which the region is named
the snow-capped peaks of the Tian Shan surrounding the lake
drawn from the Kyrgyz national flag

description: A blue field charged centrally with the regional emblem of Issyk-Kul — featuring Lake Issyk-Kul, the surrounding snowy peaks of the Tian Shan, and a sun.
history: Issyk-Kul Region surrounds the lake of the same name (the second-largest mountain lake in the world) and is one of the seven regions of Kyrgyzstan. The regional flag reflects the dominant geographical feature of the region.

features.symbolism: Lake Issyk-Kul and the surrounding Tian Shan peaks
